explicit_text: I joined the United States Navy Reserve in September 1966 because I wanted to serve my country during the Vietnam War build-up but preferred the Navy over the Air Force or Army. I attended meetings at Monroe Harbor in Chicago for about a year before being called to active duty. Joining the Reserve allowed me to avoid the draft and serve in a way I felt comfortable with.

explicit_text: In September 1967, I was sent to Treasure Island in San Francisco for about three weeks. It was an in-transit warehouse where they processed us and prepared us for our next orders. This was a key stop before I was assigned to the USS Pueblo.

explicit_text: I reported aboard the USS Pueblo on Friday, October 13th, 1967, in San Diego. The ship was an old World War II cargo ship refurbished for intelligence gathering, cramped with eighty-three people on a 200-foot vessel. I served as part of the deck crew, handling maintenance and daily chores, but the ship’s main mission was intelligence collection during the tense period of the Vietnam War and Cold War.
